metadata.note: Initial learning dataset generated from the IceBreak master archive. Meanings and examples are designed for Japanese adult English learners.

idioms.example_en: When I think of Sri Lankan food, curry is the first thing that comes to mind.

idioms.meaning_ja: 場を和ませる、会話のきっかけを作る
idioms.example_en: Asking about local food is a lovely way to break the ice.

Asking about local food is a lovely way to break the ice.

source_occurrences.title_en: The Joy of D.I.Y. (Do It Yourself)

idioms.meaning_ja: 気分を変えて、たまには
idioms.example_en: Today, I’d like to talk about Sri Lankan food — but not curry, for a change.

source_occurrences.context: Today, I’d like to talk about Sri Lankan food — but not curry, for a change.
Today, I’d like to talk about Sri Lankan food — but not curry, for a change.

idioms.example_en: Fair enough — curry is probably the best-known dish.

source_occurrences.title_en: Sri Lankan Food Beyond Curry (Multicultural History)
source_occurrences.context: And fair enough — Sri Lankan curry is absolutely wonderful.

idioms.example_en: On top of that, Sri Lanka is close to India and important sea routes.

source_occurrences.title_en: Sri Lankan Food Beyond Curry (Multicultural History)
source_occurrences.context: On top of that, it is close to India and located on important sea routes, so it has also been influenced by the Middle East and other parts of Asia.

idioms.example_en: In a way, Sri Lankan cuisine is a real melting pot.

source_occurrences.title_en: Sri Lankan Food Beyond Curry (Multicultural History)
source_occurrences.context: So, in a way, Sri Lankan cuisine is a real melting pot.

idioms.meaning_ja: 〜についても同じことが言える

source_occurrences.context: The same is true of religion.

idioms.meaning_ja: 単に〜ではなく…でもある
idioms.example_en: Food is never just food, but a living archive of history.

idioms.example_en: One of the best things about Asaka is the balance between nature and city life.

idioms.example_en: The cherry blossoms are in full bloom in spring.

idioms.meaning_ja: 人々を一つにする、集める

source_occurrences.context: A camera is not just a tool to record facts.

source_occurrences.title_en: The Joy of D.I.Y. (Do It Yourself)
source_occurrences.context: A camera is not just a tool to record facts.

idioms.example_en: When I look through the viewfinder, time seems to slow down.

source_occurrences.context: When I look through the viewfinder, time seems to slow down.
When I look through the viewfinder, time seems to slow down, and I become deeply focused on the present moment.

idioms.example_en: Learning English opens up a new window to the world.

idioms.meaning_ja: 雰囲気・方向性を決める

idioms.example_en: Looking back, my journey into technology started decades ago.

source_occurrences.context: Looking back at old photos always brings back vivid memories and emotions from that specific day.
Looking back, my journey into technology started decades ago when I got my very first computer.

idioms.example_en: No matter how complex a machine appears, it is built on logical rules.

source_occurrences.context: It taught me that no matter how complex a machine appears on the surface, it is ultimately built on logical rules and creative human thinking.

source_occurrences.title_en: A Walk in the Neighborhood Park
source_occurrences.context: It reminds us that no matter how fast-paced our lives or our technologies become, the gentle rhythm of the physical world remains unchanged.
That early experience taught me that no matter how advanced or magical modern technology appears, it is ultimately built on simple, beautifully ordered foundational rules.

idioms.example_en: Modern society puts a lot of emphasis on speaking well.
